challenges.title: Dict-Based Record Display
challenges.description: Create a dictionary called 'record' with keys 'name', 'age', and 'job' set to 'Alice', 30, and 'Engineer' respectively. Then write a function called 'display_record' that takes a record dict and returns a formatted string like 'Alice, age 30, Engineer'.
challenges.test_code:
assert record == {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30, 'job': 'Engineer'}
assert display_record({'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30, 'job': 'Engineer'}) == 'Alice, age 30, Engineer'
assert display_record({'name': 'Bob', 'age': 25, 'job': 'Designer'}) == 'Bob, age 25, Designer'
assert display_record({'name': 'Carol', 'age': 45, 'job': 'Manager'}) == 'Carol, age 45, Manager'
hints: Use curly braces to build the dict: {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30, 'job': 'Engineer'}

challenges.title: Record Database Search
challenges.description: Create a list called 'database' containing three record dicts: Alice/30/Engineer, Bob/25/Designer, Carol/45/Manager. Then write a function 'search_by_field' that takes the database list, a field name, and a value, and returns a list of all records where that field matches the value.
challenges.starter_code:
# Create a list of 3 record dicts (name, age, job)
database = []

def search_by_field(db, field, value):
    """Return list of records where record[field] == value."""
    pass
challenges.test_code:
assert len(database) == 3
assert database[0]['name'] == 'Alice'
assert database[1]['name'] == 'Bob'
assert database[2]['name'] == 'Carol'
assert search_by_field(database, 'name', 'Alice') == [{'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30, 'job': 'Engineer'}]
assert search_by_field(database, 'job', 'Designer') == [{'name': 'Bob', 'age': 25, 'job': 'Designer'}]
assert search_by_field(database, 'age', 99) == []
assert search_by_field(database, 'job', 'Engineer') == [{'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30, 'job': 'Engineer'}]
hints: Build the database as a list of dicts: [{'name': 'Alice', ...}, ...]
Use a list comprehension: [rec for rec in db if rec[field] == value]
